DEBRIEFING SUIMMARY—LT. MERWON CORR, IMPERIAL DEFECTOR

Analyst’s note: The following statements are excerpts from Alliance Intelligences post-de-
fection debriefing of Lt. Merwon Corr. As a known former ISB agent, Lt. Corr's credibility is
questionable. Agents and others utilizing this data should be aware that it may contain misin-
formation at best and outright traps at worst. In particular, Lt. Corr’s insistence that Byss is
more than the Emperor's long-rumored mysterious retreat seems sure to be an Imperial ruse.

You have to understand how dangerous the Deep Core is, even for Imperial starships. The difficulties
are twofold. One is the navigational challenges and hazards created by the natural conditions within the
region. It would be simplistic to say that the deeper you get into the Core, the more difficult the condi-
tions. While generally true, the reality is some areas are easier to travel through than others. Getting to a
well-known system on an ancient, well-known route like Empress Teta via the Koros Trunk Line is physically
possible, if more challenging than the average hyperspace jump. Getting to a world without a hyperroute
is extremely dangerous, and nearly suicidal. We lose, | mean they lose, scouts and other ships that make
regular runs through the Deep Core on a regular basis.

Even if you get past the physical limitations, you have the Imperial patrols and bureaucracy to deal with.
It's not just the natural barriers that keep people out. The Empire set up the Deep Core Security Zone
specifically to keep anyone from poking around. You can get in, of course, provided you obtain the right
paperwork, permits, and permissions. And you better get them. Anyone found within the Zone without it
is arrested immediately. Drop into the wrong system without a proper access code, you might not survive
long enough to argue your point. Of course, most people can’t pass the background checks, by design
more often than not.

Want to go on your own anyway? Making a jump without using an approved route risks smashing into a
sophisticated mine or other hazard scattered about the hyperlanes and likely arrival points designed to wipe
out unauthorized ships. Random patrols, scout ships, and swarms of probe droids keep an eye on the safest
routes. Not coincidentally, they also keep the Imperial astrogation charts up-to-date, which is no small feat.

Why all the security? You've heard the stories. Take your pick. The Empire wants any resources it finds
for itself. The worst Imperial prisons in the galaxy are there. The Emperor's hidden retreat holds many
secrets. Maybe the Emperor doesn’t want anyone going near the supposed ancient homeworld of the
misguided Jedi. Maybe he’s protecting the galaxy from the evils of the dark side of the Force. Maybe he's
training them himself. After all, you do know that the Citadel Inquisitorius, home to those evil, Force-
wielding terrors, is deep in the Deep Core. They may not be Jedi, but they know some of their tricks,
that’s for certain.

| think the stories are all true, after a fashion. I've seen too many ambitious and/or gullible nobles make
some sort of pilgrimage out to Byss and never return. Sure, Byss is real. The Emperor's private play-
ground, as near as | can tell. No, | didn’t bring proof. There isn't any. Someone will probably kill me just for
acknowledging its presence. If half of the stories I've heard are true, well, I'd rather fight with the Rebels
than become a mindless slave or pawn in the Emperor’s schemes. Less than a pawn, really.

Here’s some hard evidence you will believe. This data chip details half a dozen Imperial prison worlds in
and near the Deep Core. Not only that, this datapad holds the permits and everything you need to look
like an approved supply freighter. It won't last long, as the codes change all the time, but if you hurry, it
might hold long enough to rescue your friends. What friends? That's what this list holds. Rebel agents and
sympathizers. Go ahead, look at it. | bet there are names on there you didn't even know were missing yet,
or thought were dead long ago.


### The Core Worlds

THE CORE WORLDS

he Core Worlds region is the governmental and cul-

tural center of the galaxy. The Core Worlds were once
the heart of the Republic. Now they form the seemingly
unbreakable political and commercial backbone of the
Galactic Empire. The region consists of a semicircular
ring of star systems around the north, east, and south
sides of the Deep Core. Every major hyperlane starts in
or intersects with the Core Worlds.

CORE WORLD DOMINANCE

The Core Worlds are among the most ancient civiliza-
tions of the Republic. From the central worlds, explo-
ration radiated outward toward the rim of the galaxy.
With it came governmental, societal, and economic
expansion that funneled vast amounts of wealth back
to the Core Worlds. These densely populated planets
are among the most powerful in the galaxy.

The Clone Wars shattered the Core Worlds’ long-held
notions that they were largely beyond the reach of major
wars or widespread violent conflict, unlike “less civilized
regions.” Chancellor Palpatine manipulated and used
their fear of war to build support for his vision of peace
through military strength and a dominant central gov-
ernment. The citizens welcomed the safety and stability
of the new Empire, and became its strongest supporters
in the galaxy. The rest of the galaxy regards the Core
Worlds’ populace as rich, snobbish, and elitist. Imperial
rule and favoritism only increased this perception and
reality. Arrogance is particularly prevalent on human-
dominated worlds, due to the Empire’s human-centric
edicts and views. Most Core World citizens actively sup-
port the Imperial cause and government.

The Core Worlds, and Imperial Center (Coruscant)
In particular, are the greatest driver of the Imperial
bureaucracy and war machine. Core Worlders, like
most dedicated Imperial supporters, tend to ‘turn @
blind eye toward the darker side of the Empire. In fact,

they remain ignorant of its worst evils, which are hid-
den and obscured by the Emperor, his aides, and the
Imperial bureaucratic machine. Disturbing, unsavory,
and outright evil events that do come to light are dis-
missed as dissident Rebel lies and propaganda. Impe-
rial viewpoints are further expressed and supported by
organizations like COMPNOR (Commission for the Pres-
ervation of the New Order). COMPNOR simultaneously
Suppresses anti-lmperial sentiments, and pushes the
Emperor's social and governmental attitudes and goals.

Given the region's attitudes, it’s not surprising that
the Rebel Alliance finds it difficult to build public sup-
port for its efforts in the Core Worlds. With a general
population that is all too happy to turn Rebel sympa-
thizers over to the Imperial authorities, security and
secrecy is of the utmost importance to Rebel opera-
tives. Open support of Rebel or anti-lmperial activities
Is swiftly crushed by Imperial security and the military.
ISB agents and other police authorities relentlessly
pursue any hint of Rebel activity. Core Worlders are
horrified at even the slightest hint or accusation of
being a Rebel sympathizer, fearing the inevitable social
and Imperial repercussions.

NOTABLE LOCATIONS

The following are areas of interest within the Core
Worlds, at or before the rise of the Empire.

Abregado-rae: Located in the Borderlands region of
the Core Worlds, Abregado-rae is a center of manufac-
turing and trade. It marks the start of the Rimma Trade
Route but is not especially influential in galactic affairs.
This is partly due to the fact that its operations and
attitudes are more like those of an Outer Rim world
than a Core World, including a rich smuggling tradition.

Alderaan: Alderaan was one of the original worlds of
the Republic, a home to early human colonists. The
planet was destroyed by the Death Star in an attempt
to intimidate the Galactic citizenry into submission
before the Empire, and to wrest the location of a secret
Rebel base from Princess Leia Organa. The rocky, shat-
tered remains of the planet are called The Graveyard.
See page 474 for more information.

Caamas: The homeworld of the pacifist, scholarly
Caamasi species was completely devastated by orbital
bombardment by mysterious forces several years ago.
The Caamasi still adhered to the Old Republic ideals,
whicn brought them into direct conflict with the Empire.
The survivors relocated to other worlds, including the
doomed Alderaan.

Chandrila: A pleasant world known for commerce and
political candor. Its inhabitants are often philosophical,
and the planet avoided major war for millennia. It is the
homeworld of Mon Mothma, founder and leader of the
Alliance to Restore the Republic. See page 476 for
more information.

Corellia: Corellia is the beneficiary and founder of two
major hyperlanes that intersect the system, the Corel-
lian Run and Corellian Trade Spine. It is an unusual plan-
etary system, consisting of five inhabited worlds, two
of which orbit the ancient and mysterious Centerpoint
Station. Three species call Corellia home: humans, Sel-
onians, and Drall. Corellia is famous for its shipyards
and the free-spirited attitude of its human citizens.
Despite this, the Empire keeps strict control over the
system, in part due to the importance of the Corellian
Engineering Corporation and its vital shipyards.

Corulag: Corulag is dedicated to Emperor Palpatine’s
New Order. It is home to the prestigious Corulag Acad-
emy, a highly acclaimed and sought-after military
school. Bright young cadets from the Core Worlds seek
to jumpstart their promising careers, spurred on and
guided by their noble families, political insiders, and
corporate sponsors.

Dentaal: The population of Dentaal was recently deci-
mated by the Candorian plague. Billions of lives were
lost to the incurable disease. The Empire blames the
Rebellion for unleashing a biowar agent that went far
beyond expectations, while the Rebellion blames the
Empire for secretly releasing the disease in retaliation

CORE WORLD INTELLIGENCE UPDATE TO THE
REBEL CIVIL GOVERNMENT

The destruction of the Death Star and Alder-
aan had a profound effect on the Core Worlds.
As they both benefit from and bear the brunt of
Imperial power, reactions differ somewhat from
system to system. For those firmly in the Imperial
camp, the destruction of Alderaan was horrifying,
yet strangely accepted as required to keep the
Galactic peace. Many of these worlds viewed Al-
deraan as a home of troublemakers and traitors.
some likely felt that they got what they deserved.

While the Core is the center of Imperial power, there
are still worlds that oppose and resist Imperial rule
as best they can. Many of those worlds now fear
heavier Imperial crackdowns. While the threat of
planetary annihilation is gone along with the Death
star, the Empire has proved that it will not hesitate
to decimate a world it deems an enemy. Orbital
bombardment can provide more than enough de-
struction if the Empire decides to make an example
of another world. Some systems that were on the
edge of Rebel support have been cowed into taking a
few steps back. They might still support the Rebel-
lion, but not publicly. The risk is too great.

On top of this, the Imperials have noticeably in-
creased security throughout the Core Worlds.
Organizations like COMPNOR have expanded their
activities, with growing public displays of Impe-
rial support. Suspected Rebel sympathizers are
hunted down ruthlessly. On most Imperial worlds,
Rebel allies are shunned at best, reported to the
ISB at worst. Though many citizens privately ac-
knowledge that they are sickened and horrified by
the destruction of Alderaan, they will not speak
out, for fear of reprisal and arrest.

for the planet’s rejection of Imperial rule. The Rebels
have the inside story, as former Imperial storm com-
mando leader Crix Madine defected to the Rebel Alli-
ance over his role in the events.

Duro: Duro is the homeworld of the Duros species.
The Duros were among the earliest spacefaring soci-
eties, predating even the modern hyperdrive. They
originated and blazed a great number of hyperroutes,
including the early portion of the Corellian Trade Spine.
They developed and colonized the systems they dis-
covered to great economic effect. Eventually, the
surface of their homeworld became overwhelmed by
industry and sprawling automated corporate farms.
Due to high pollution and terrible conditions, the Duros
live in orbital cities or elsewhere offworld. The Empire
continues to strip what little remains of the planet's
natural resources for use in Duros’s shipyards.

Imperial Center (Coruscant): Imperial Center is
the newly renamed traditional Republic capital world
of Coruscant. It is the center of galactic culture and

a) “

REBEL ROUSER

It's true! Rebel starfighters struck an Imperial
convoy out of Balmorra! Blew a nice new Imperial
cruiser to pieces. That's the third attack in three
weeks. I'd say the Rebels are getting serious, with
that kind of military hardware flying around, mak-
ing open attacks. If they can blow up the Death
Star with only starfighters, I'd say any target Is in
serious trouble.

| bet it’s a build up to the Ghorman protests. Ever
since Alderaan, things have only become more
restless around here. Plenty of angry people to
join the throngs of protesters. Sure it's danger-

ous, but are we going to put up with open death
and destruction from our Core World masters yet
again? When is it going to stop? Sure I'm going.
You should too. You know, strength in numbers.

No? We have to show the people we wont live
under a government that destroys—no, obliter-
ates—its own worlds. You know Tarkin was be-
hind Alderaan, right? Just like Ghorman. Sure,
he’s dead now, thanks to the Rebels, but there are
plenty of Imperials just waiting for that next pro-
motion to Moff or Grand Moff or whatever he was.

Oh, so another protest wont change much, Is
that it? You want to do more? Seriously? Good.
| have a deal for you. You're a decent pilot, right?
No? Mechanic? That's fine, too. | know exactly
who needs a few good mechanics to repair a few,
um, damaged starfighters. Interested? Low pay,
high danger, improved moral footing sound good
to you? Great. Come with me.

,
|

influence, as well as the origin point on Republic and
Imperial navigation charts. It is a dense, multi-level,
planet-wide city, with the dregs of society far below
the dominant and prominent surface world. See page
479 for more information.

Kuat: Kuat is vital to the Empire as home of the stra-
tegically important Kuat Drive Yards and builder of
the iconic and dominating Imperial Star Destroyer,
as well as other ships and vessels. Kuat is completely
ringed by an orbital stardock for starship manufactur-
ing, which produces thousands of ships a year. Hun-
dreds of millions of workers inhabit the orbital ring. The
Empire maintains high security around the system at
all times, requiring ships to approach and leave the sys-
tem through specific hyperspace routes, usually routed
through nearby systems as waypoints to deter Rebel
attack and other disruptions.

Ralltiir: Ralltiir is home to an extensive banking system
that influences important financial markets in the gal-
axy. Various agents and actions worked to steer these
markets toward Imperial support. However, after Rebel
sympathizers tried to counter this, the Empire invaded
and seized direct control. In response, the Rebels target
the system with raids and other covert operations.

Rendili: Rendili is home to one of the galaxy’s larg-
est starship producers, Rendili StarDrive. Rendili sup-
ported the Separatists during the Clone Wars, but the
effort was limited by its own home fleet's loyalty to the
Republic. Chancellor Palpatine manipulated the situa-
tion to take such fleets under more direct governmen-
tal control. Rendili StarDrive also lost out on Imperial
contracts as a result.

‘THE COLONIES

he Colonies form the first ring of major galactic explo-

ration and expansion beyond the Core Worlds. The
Colonies are the second most densely populated region
after the Core Worlds themselves, and magnitudes
greater than the galaxy at large. The Colonies extend
in a semicircular pattern around the similarly explored
Core Worlds region.

Settlement of the Colonies started before the estab-
lishment of the Republic. Explorers and colonists
extended the power and ambition of the Core Worlds
from which they came. Primitive generation snips pre-
dated hyperspace-capable craft, making the coloniza-
tion effort all the more remarkable. Due to the limita-
tions of travel and communications, many worlds broke
away from the original rulers within a few generations.
The ongoing conflicts between the Colonies and their
homeworlds proved to be a driving force in the estab-
lishment of a galactic government.

COLONIAL POWER

Though the region still struggles for the prestige it feels
it deserves, the Colonies hold some of the wealthiest
worlds in the galaxy. Compared to the outer territories,
the Colonies are culturally advanced and an economic
powerhouse in their own right, though the Core is
reluctant to ever acknowledge this fact. The region has
been a source of political strife and change through-
out the history of the Republic. The Colonies believe
themselves to be at the economic forefront, combining
power and wealth with raw materials needed through-
out the galaxy.

With the development of the Colonies came the
advancement of galactic standards and laws aimed
toward curbing abuses of power both governmental
and corporate. Much of this was due to the fact that
major galactic struggles often erupted from conflicts
with and within the Colonies themselves. Due to this
reputation, the Empire keeps careful watch over the
region. They deal harshly with any troublesome areas,
seeking to prevent any antigovernment sentiment from
growing into a major threat to Imperial power.

Despite the manipulation of contracts to keep the
region both happy and under Imperial control, a grow-
ing resentment towards the government continues to
build. Imperial crackdowns like the Ghorman atrocity
add dramatically, if quietly, to these sentiments. The
Rebellion is actively exploiting these feelings by deploy-
ing agents and military resources into the region. While
ramping up clandestine efforts, the Rebellion brings
more citizens to its secret training facilities every day.

NOTABLE LOCATIONS

The following are areas of interest within the Colonies
at or before the rise of the Empire.

Arkania: A cold, tundra-covered world located
between the Perlemian Trade Route and the Hydian
Way, Arkania is the homeworld of the native Arkanians
and their genetically modified offshoots, who are
treated as lesser beings. The Arkanians are scientifi-
cally minded, but often unscrupulous in their dealings
and experiments.

Balmorra: Balmorra is of critical importance to the
Imperial military. It is a heavily industrialized world,
best known for producing weapons and vehicles for the
Imperial army. It is one of the primary manufacturing
locations for the AT-ST walker.

Byblos: A major manufacturer of military equipment,
Byblos is a heavily populated world on the Corellian
Run. The planet's unique architecture features enor-
mous towers of up to 5,000 levels, with each tower
dedicated to a specific function. The world is also
known for Byblos Drive Yards, which produces air-
speeders and other flying vehicles.

Cato Neimoidia: Cato Neimoidia is best known as a
Neimoidian “purse world.” It is an independent colony,
and once was one of the wealthiest planets in the gal-
axy. It served as a major Separatist stronghold in the
Clone Wars, until it was captured by Republic forces. It
is Known for its unusual bridge cities, which are under-
slung from enormous natural rock arches. Though it is
uncommon, both the Empire and the Rebellion occa-
sionally conduct covert operations, and counter opera-
tions, involving local underworld figures,


### The Inner Rim

Commenor: Commenor is well-known for its indepen-
dent spirit. The Rebel Alliance established a secret
Starfighter base on its largest moon, Folor. Rogue
Squadron fighter pilot Tycho Celchu defected to the
Alliance at Commenor.

Devaron: Devaron, the homeworld of the Devaroni-
ans, is located near the Corellian Trade Spine. It is a
mountainous world covered with deep valleys, lakes,
and major rivers. Devaronian females tend to remain
on their homeworld, while the males wander the planet
or the galaxy at large.

Fondor: Fondor’s orbital shipyards make it another
important Imperial military manufacturing complex. The
shipyard is responsible for building Darth Vader's mon-
strous new flagship, the Super Star Destroyer Executor.
The construction of the ship caused infighting and intrigue
among Imperial admirals and rivals of Darth Vader.

Ghorman: Located along the Rimma Trade Route,
Ghorman is best known as the site of a major public
Imperial atrocity. In response to an antitax demonstra-
tion, the Imperial commander Wilhuff Tarkin landed his
ship (sent to collect the taxes) on top of the demon-
strators. Tarkin was promoted to the rank of Moff as a
reward. However, the brutal event serves as a rallying
cry against the Empire throughout the Colonies and by
Rebel agents and other dissidents across the galaxy.

Giju: Giju is the homeworld of the Herglics and the cen-
ter of their pre-Republic empire. They once colonized
and still control systems light years away from their

THE INNER RIM

0 riginally called just “the Rim” when first opened for
exploration and settlement in the early Republic, the
Inner Rim gained its current name after scouts contin-
ued into the Expanded Rim, now known as the Expan-
sion Region. The shape of the populated area is roughly
semicircular, with a northeastern bulge and narrowed
eastern zone in the Slice, between the Trellen Trade
Route and the Corellian Run.

The Inner Rim marks the original boundary of civi-
lized, explored space. In its early days, it was the great
frontier: raw and rugged. Corporate concerns and rapid
colonization quickly pushed the frontier further out,
actually distorting the shape of the Inner Rim region in
the process. Those avoiding the corporate exploitation
of the Expanded Rim also avoided the eastern edge,
creating the northeastern bulge. It wasn't long before
the Inner Rim was well within the realm of Republic
commerce and politics.

THE GALAXY
AGE OF REBELLION

ce

homeworld, stretching out to the Inner Rim. Their influ-
ence extended along the Rimma Trade Route, and they
were one of the first species to join the Republic. The
Herglics initially resisted the establishment of Imperial
rule. Their efforts were short-lived, however, as Impe-
rial forces brutally took control. The Herglics have since
submitted to their fate as virtual slaves to the Empire.

Mrlisst: Mrisst is a swampy planet known for its pres-
tigious universities, scholars, and scientists. Their
research into cloaking technology has garnered Impe-
rial grants for further study, but has produced no work-
ing models as of yet.

Namadii Corridor: The Namadii Corridor is an impor-
tant hyperspace route between Imperial Center (Corus-
cant) and Bilbringi in the Inner Rim, though it extends
to Namadii in the Mid Rim.

Neimoidia: Neimoidia is the Neimoidian homeworld,
originally colonized by the Duros. The world is regularly
overshadowed by the nearby Core Worlds, an irritation
endured for centuries by the Neimoidians. Since back-
ing the Separatists and Trade Federation during the
Clone Wars, they have receded in galactic importance
following their defeat and the rise of the Empire.

Procopia: Procopia, located in the southern reaches
of the Colonies, is the capital world and heart of the old
Tapani Empire. It now serves as capital of the Tapani
sector. Procopia’s inhabitants, the Tapani, still exert
economic and political influence throughout the area.

INNER TURMOIL

The Inner Rim holds a great variety of worlds, many in
major agricultural and industrial roles. Within the Slice,
population density is similar to that of the Colonies,
but it drops off quickly in other areas. Culturally speak-
ing, the Inner Rim is the dividing line between the core
of the galaxy and the outlying sectors. As such, the res-
idents are a bit unique, looking up at the Core regions,
and down on their ever distant neighbors. Like those
in the Colonies, the citizens of the Inner Rim harbor
a well-known undercurrent of jealousy for their neign-
bors closer to the core.

Despite its distance from the Galactic Core, the Inner
Rim worlds produce some of the most sought-after
goods in the galaxy. While it is an important industrial
zone, the Inner Rim holds two main sources of critical
healing substances. These are Manaan for kolto (espe-
cially in the ancient Republic), and Thyferra for bacta.
With the rising Galactic Civil War, Thyferra in particular
naturally attracts Rebel interests, as securing a reliable

MARTIAL LOGISTICS

Well of course the system was under martial law. fj
This is the Inner Rim. It’s a restless place, and |
the Empire doesn't ever forget it. If you want to
operate here, you best get used to it. Otherwise,

ll ship you right back to the Outer Rim, where

you can continue your suddenly stagnating Rebel
career at a, oh, | don’t know, maybe a Tatooine
listening post or something.

Now, since you've gone and stirred up Bestine yet
again, we'll have to let things cool off there before
another group of defectors shows up to join up. You
don’t have any humans on your crew, right? Good. |
mean, | dont care, but for this mission, that’s help-
ful. | need you to make a bacta run out to Thyferra.

No, it isn't that simple. We don’t work with the
cartel, when we can avoid it. That means sneak-
ing around. We've made contact with one of the
Vratix resistance groups. They're willing to sell
us bacta, but they really don’t like humans. It’s
a problem, sure, but were working on it. Well,
slowly working on it. The point is, | need you to fly
in, buy enough bacta to fill your ship's hold, and
bring it back here. If the cartel figures out what's
going on, theyre every bit as bad as the Imperi-
als. Theyre often better equipped and better paid,
which makes them better motivated. If they catch
you, youll be lucky if you even need a bacta tank
to recover. Many who cross the cartel end up on
the wrong side of a blaster. Think of it this way, ev-
ery bit of that bacta you bring back helps another
Rebel live to fight another day. Maybe even you.

and affordable source of bacta is critical to any large-
scale war effort.

The Galactic Civil War is only the most recent of the
regularly occurring wars and conflicts throughout the
Inner Rim’s history. Some of the earliest secessionist
fighting in the Clone Wars erupted at the Gotal home
system of Antar. The Inner Rim harbored the closest
border of continuous Separatist space towards the
core regions. The region was centered on the intersec-
tion of the Rimma Trade Route and the Corellian Trade
Spine, but stretched all the way to the Outer Rim.

The Imperials have neither forgotten, forgiven, nor
completely trust systems with a Separatist past. Inner
Rim worlds are often under martial law, and they are

regularly threatened with it. The increasingly oppressive
conditions provide an increasingly fertile region for
Rebel agents in search of recruits and sympathizers.
As the citizenry grows restless, significant numbers
are relocating to the less-regulated Expansion Region.
Others try to counter the Empire on their own terms, at
various levels of dissent and resistance.

NOTABLE LOCATIONS

The following are areas of interest within the Inner Rim,
at or before the rise of the Empire.

Antar: The Gotal homeworld of Antar IV has a highly
unusual orbit around its gas giant star, which causes
extreme variations in the amount of light striking the
planet on any given day. The Gotals are well-adapted
to their unique conditions. There is generally no orga-
nized central government. Given the planet's Separat-
ist past, The Imperials closely watch the system for
any signs of Rebel activity or sympathetic actions, a
task made more difficult by the decentralized nature
of Gotal society,

Bestine: Once regarded as yet another nondescript
colony world, Bestine now serves as a rallying point
for the Rebellion, as well as a demonstration of Impe-
rial power to the rest of the galaxy. Several years ago,
the Empire forcibly relocated the established colony in
order to make way for a new naval shipyard, making
and breaking many promises in the process. The sys-
tem became a hotbed of overt Rebel activity, starting
before the Battle of Yavin. The system has seen spo-
radic starship battles between Rebel ships and Impe-
rial Star Destroyers. Defections to the Rebellion are
also more common at Bestine than elsewhere, yielding
such important resources as the starship Rand Ecliptic
and pilot Biggs Darklighter.

Bilbringi: Bilbringi is a lifeless system rich in raw materi-
als needed for starship construction. It is home to a very
well-defended Imperial orbital shipyard, and is the Inner
Rim end of the Namadii Corridor hyperspace route.

Denon: Denon is a completely urbanized world. It is
a lesser version of Imperial Center (Coruscant), with
much less power and prestige. An increasingly loyal
Imperial world, it seeks to improve its position within
the Empire. Its efforts have not gone unnoticed, but
rather stand out in the restless Inner Rim. There has
been a slow-but-noticeable increase in Imperial mili-
tary traffic through the system lately. Rebel agents find
it more like a Core World when they try to recruit or to
establish operations on the planet.

Hapes: Hapes is the name of a cluster of stars and an
ancient world not far from the Perlemian Trade Route.
Isolated from surrounding space by gas and ionized
particles, it is largely autonomous, standing apart
from the Empire, as it did the Republic. As it is mainly
focused on its own internal political affairs, it draws
little interest from the Rebels or the Empire.

Manaan: Manaan is the water-covered homeworld of
the aquatic Selkath species. Since the Old Republic,
Manaan has been the only known source of kolto, a
strong healing substance. In modern times, Manaan is
better known as a resort world, if not a popular one.
The Empire seized control of the world years ago, and
rumors of secret Imperial activity persist.

Myrkr: Myrkr is an extremely obscure world, barely
known as a smuggler’s hideout even in the best-informed
underworld circles. The trees have a natural metal con-
tent that make sensor scans difficult. The planet's great-
est secret is that it is home to the ysalamiri. These crea-
tures naturally repel the Force, completely defeating the
Force abilities of Force-sensitive individuals.

Onderon: Onderon is covered in wild, lethal jungles.
It has a single huge, fortified settlement called Iziz to
hold back the aggressive native beasts. It also has an
ancient reputation for savagery and evil. One of Onder-
on’s moons, Dxun, holds the tomb of the ancient Sith
Lord Freedon Nadd, and at certain times, it actually
passes close enough to Onderon for an atmospheric
bridge between the worlds. The Imperial Army has a
large ordnance center on the planet, and the Rebels,
not coincidentally, have successfully bolstered a resis-
tance movement within |ziz. Clashes between the two
forces occur with increasing frequency and intensity.

Taanab: Taanab Is an agricultural world and exporter of
foodstuffs. The planet is most famous for the recently
reported “Battle of Taanab,’ where Lando Calrissian cre-
atively defeated pirate raiders who menaced the world.

Thyferra: Thyferra is the homeworld of the Vratix spe-
cies, the inventors of bacta. With the rise of the Empire,
two corporations, Xucphra and Zaltin, negotiated a
deal with the Imperial government to control virtually
all of the galaxy’s bacta. These human-owned compa-
nies that now dominate Thyferra’s government are col-
lectively known as the Bacta Cartel. The Rebel Alliance
routinely clashes with the Bacta Cartel in and out of the
system. Conflicts range from minor skirmishes to open
combat, such as Starfighter raids, as the Rebels seek to
protect their bacta sources on Thyferra and beyond.
The cartels also keep the Vratix as virtual slaves. Some
Vratix are now resisting, but many dissidents blame the
humans for their problems.

Yag’Dhul: Yag’Dhul is the bizarre homeworld of the
Givin species. The planet's three moons and complex
orbit generate extreme tidal forces that expose por-
tions of the world to hard vacuum. The Givin naturally
resist such conditions, and became shipbuilders and
mathematicians as a result.

Zeltros: The homeworld of the Zeltrons, an attractive,
freewheeling, celebratory species best Known for its
never-ending desire and ability to have a good time.
Invasions invariably fail when the occupiers are over-
whelmed by the Zeltrons’ natural pheromones. Impe-
rial officials have difficulty enforcing Imperial law under
these conditions, but as the Zeltrons are pacifists, they
neither pose a threat to nor possess much desire to
join the Rebels.


### The Expansion Region

THE TROUBLE WITH EXPANDING THE ALLIANCE

At a galactic scale, |'m telling you, the Rebels are still nothing more than a nuisance. Sure, they destroyed
the Death Star, and that can’t be ignored, but where is the widespread public support? Where are the
planets flocking to their side? | mean, the Separatists had more political pull over the galaxy during the
Clone Wars. Granted, they had the big corporations on their side, as well as Senators, plus they could buy
armies of battle droids...but they still turned entire sectors and important star systems to their cause.
You'd think that some of those systems would still be up for escaping the Empire, at the very least.

| grant you that there are troublesome systems or sectors. Take our own Expansion Region, for example.
So the Rebels destroy the occasional Imperial starship, or disrupt sector-wide communications for a few
hours. What does that change? The Empire has thousands upon thousands of ships. They even build them
here. The loss of one vessel, or one squadron, or one fleet hardly diminishes their galactic power. I'm just
saying that | don't see any sign of massive star fleets or invading Rebel armies anywhere. | don’t like the
Empire any more than you, but opposing it is just...suicide.

Again, look at the Expansion Region. The Separatists were strong here. The economy is terrible. There are
a lot of reasons to hate the Empire. There are ample places to hide just about any kind of facility you'd like
to set up. Pick an abandoned mining world just about anywhere, and you have a ready-made hidden base.
Give people paying jobs, and they'll follow whoever has the credits, | bet.

Yes, | know. I've heard the same rumors and seen the same news stories. The Rebels might be doing all of
that. But here's the point. Is it enough to significantly change anything, or are they just making everyone's
lives miserable and, frankly, putting us all at risk? | don’t know. It seems like it plays right into the Empire's
hands, giving them reasons to crack down on all of us. I’m no fan of the Empire, but the Rebels are going to
have to show a lot more public support before | go along with them.

THE EXPANSION REGION

ECONOMIC FLUX

Eventually , the Expanded Rim was renamed the Expan-

ne Expansion Region is one of the galaxy’s primary
regions for manufacturing and heavy industry. The
region underwent extreme corporate development and

extensive mining over the centuries. With a significant
percentage of the mines played out, the region has
suffered economically for the past few centuries. The
Expansion Region has an irregular shape, with a very
thin band in the northern reaches of the region and a
wider bulge in the eastern and southeastern areas. The
population density of the region is far less than that of
the Core Worlds, or even the Inner Rim.

While the Inner Rim was still developing, explorers
and corporate opportunists surged into the region
known at the time as the Expanded Rim. A boom was
well underway before the area was officially opened for
colonization. Corporations quickly established major
mining colonies and industrial complexes on the worlds
they seized. The operations were so disruptive they
distorted the shape of the Inner Rim region, producing
its northeastern bulge.

sion Region, and further exploration and development
became an experiment in corporate governance and
control. Mega-corporations and guilds controlled
entire star systems and clusters, efficiently stripping
them of any usable materials, which they shipped back
to the inner regions or processed them in local indus-
trial and manufacturing complexes.

Wars and conflicts in the early years changed the
exploration and settlement efforts, pushing them fur-
ther westward in the region. Eventually, these more
successful and diverse sectors sought to culturally dis-
tance themselves, calling their area the Trailing Sectors.

The northern sectors lagged in development. In
response, the Republic granted their development and
governing rights to the Expansionist Oligarchy. Unfor-
tunately, the corporate conglomerates of the Oligarchy
abused their power, reducing the citizenry to near-pov-
erty conditions. Once the scandal broke, the Senate

THE GALAXY
ACE OF REBELLION

=


### The Mid Rim

removed the Oligarchy from power, in a move that has
replayed across the Expansion Region over the centu-
ries. Corporate control gave way to Republic represen-
tation, but the companies still exert influence over the
region. This experiment eventually led to the develop-
ment of the Corporate Sector, far out in the Outer Rim.

In recent centuries, the mines of the original colonies
in the Slice region played out, plunging the area into an
unstoppable economic decline. The systems and cor-
porations struggle with what remains, and conditions
are unlikely to improve. The economic problems com-
bined with traditional corporate power primed large
areas within the region for joining the Separatists dur-
ing the Clone Wars.

After the war, the Empire moved to stabilize the
economic situation by creating major staging areas
for Imperial cargo and military fleets. This included
efforts to relocate portions of the population, as well as
take advantage of Inner Rim refugees who fled to the
Expansion Region in hopes of finding better opportuni-
ties and less oppressive conditions. They only contrib-
uted to the economic issues, especially employment.

While the economic revival bolsters the Empire's
Standing with the population, the Rebel Alliance still
finds plenty of disgruntled citizens willing to reject the
Empire once and for all. The Rebels exploit the long-
abandoned systems and mines for bases and training
facilities. They also target corporations friendly to the
Empire, though they must strike a careful balance as
any action that reduces economic opportunities also
hurts their cause in the view of the public.

NOTABLE LOCATIONS

The following are areas of interest within the Expansion
Region, at or before the rise of the Empire.

THE MID RIM

he Mid Rim opened for colonization after the Expan-

sion Region's booming growth subsided. The Mid
Rim is Known as a relatively peaceful and somewhat
boring region of the galaxy. Its eastern areas (within
the Slice) developed first, with the northern and south-
ern regions growing significantly after the opening of
the Hydian Way. The region’s eastern side is signifi-
cantly wider than the northern and southern portions,
while the northern area is barely connected to the rest
of the region. The eastern edge borders Hutt Space,
which has intruded into the Mid Rim at many points
throughout history. Population density is significantly
lower than in any of the interior regions.

THE GALAXY
AGE OF REBELLION

ad

Bacrana: Bacrana is one of the Imperial Navy's largest
regional staging areas, and home to one of the interstel-
lar corporations that control many of the local systems.
The Rebels are highly active in the sector, having estab-
lished a secret military base, training facility, and multiple
safe houses there. The system and sector have become
major Rebel flashpoints in the Expansion Region.

Cathar: Cathar is the homeworld of the species of the
Same name, feline beings who live in large city-trees. It
is best known for suffering a devastating attack during
the ancient Mandalorian Wars, before the world joined
the Republic.

Dorin: Dorin is the home planet of the Kel Dor spe-
cies. Black holes restrict hyperspace travel in this area.
Dorin’s atmosphere is toxic to most offworld species,
and the Kel Dor themselves must wear breath masks
and protective eyewear offworld.

Iktotchon: |ktotchon is the homeworld of the Iktot-
chi species. Known for their pre-cognitive abilities,
they foresaw the conditions of the new Empire. Many
returned to Iktotchon to escape the dark times, and
new orbital colonies were built to accommodate those
returning to the system. The Empire blockaded the sys-
tem, but neither side seems willing to openly press the
issue. However, Iktotchi who remain outside the system
are increasingly joining the Rebel Alliance.

Kinyen: Kinyen is the homeworld of the Gran species.
They once maintained a meticulously balanced and
peaceful civilization, but they restricted it to the Gran
only. When they resisted Imperial orders to open their
world, they were met by a devastating orbital bom-
bardment. The incident turned many Gran to join or
sympathize with the Rebellion.

Tierfon: Tierfon holds an active secret underground
Rebel starfighter outpost housing a small number of
X-wing fighters. The Tierfon Yellow Aces squadron oper-
ated from the base prior to the Battle of Yavin, with
several pilots participating in that famous engagement.

Unlike its Coreward neighbor, the Mid Rim enjoyed
a relatively organized and lengthy colonization period.
While corporations plundered the Expansion Region,
a different type of colonist arrived in the Mid Rim.
Some were idealistic organizations, such as religious
groups or ambitious, radical political groups. The Mid
Rim enjoyed effective governance and general security,
thanks in no small part to the simultaneous opening of
the Outer Rim to colonization. Those avoiding or flee-
ing Republic interference opted for the more distant
Outer Rim, bypassing Mid Rim worlds completely.

MIDDLE OF THE PACK

Much of the Mid Rim remains unexplored, or only
nominally surveyed. Its systems tend to be clustered
around the major hyperlanes. While the region has
a reputation for relative peace, the vast open areas
allow pirates to operate more easily than in the central
regions. As a result, pirates have been a major problem
in the Mid Rim for centuries.

During the Clone Wars, much of the eastern Mid Rim
territory within the Slice was dominated by Separat-
ists. The conflict allowed a temporary incursion of Hutt
influence and control. Some areas remained contested
by the two factions for the duration of the war. The
Separatists heavily influenced travel in the Mid Rim,
holding portions of the Perlemian Trade Route, Corel-
lian Run, and the entirety of the Rimma Trade Route as
it passed through the Mid Rim.

Despite the region’s substantial Separatist influence
and support during the war, it has been remarkably
loyal to the Empire, due to the large areas in the north-
ern and southern reaches that remained loyal to the
Republic. Imperial efforts to crack down on piracy easily
garner the Support of the Mid Rim citizenry. The Empire
has offered relative stability following years of war.

NOTABLE LOCATIONS

The followings are areas of interest within the Mid Rim,
at or before the rise of the Empire.

Ando: Ando |s a water world and home to the Aqualish
species, which thrives on its small islands and float-
ing cities. Once a Separatist world, the Aqualish who
live there are still restless and chafe under the Empire,
which controls the world as a police state. Uprisings
still occur on occasion but are always swiftly broken up
by the Imperial overlords.

Bimmisaari: Bimmisaari
is the homeworld of the
Bimms, two separate {but
peaceful and cooperative) =
species that share the same vf
name. One species is native -
to the planet, while the other

A =

BOTHAN SPYNET INNER RIM STABILITY RE-
PORT EXCERPT

The stability of commerce and peace throughout
the Inner Rim continues to be threatened first and
foremost by the region's traditional pirate men-
ace. Imperial crackdowns tend to follow a pat-
tern where a planetary governor or, until recently,
an Imperial Senator, complains loudly enough to
cause local political problems. Regional Imperial
task forces are regularly reassigned and moved
from one hotspot to another. While this helps
quell the politicians and bureaucrats, it only rarely
completely eliminates a pirate threat.

This behavior continues to provide a method to sub-
tly influence the orders and distribution of region-
wide Imperial forces. Relocation of some Imperial
forces is possible by boosting the appearance of
pirate attacks, or boosting the political pressure
Calling to counter the attacks. With proper plan-
ning, it may be possible to orchestrate the addition
or removal of ships to or from certain sectors or
systems, when advantageous to our purposes to
do so. Additionally, the pirates themselves remain
open to manipulation through our traditional meth-
ods, though we could and should use that power to
influence Imperial activity, per above.

A second threat to stability and security is the grow-
Ing Rebel activity in the region. Thanks in part to Im-
perial success against the pirates, the Rebellion is
slow to take hold here, as elsewhere. However, with
continued direction from us, both explicit and subtle,
that should increase greatly over the coming months.

from the Galactic Civil War, thanks in part to their
relative isolation and remote location.

Bothawui: Bothawui is the homeworld of the
S=— Bothans and the center of Bothan Space. Some
believe the Bothan Spynet contributes to the
Mid Rim’s peacefulness and stability. Officially,
Bothawui is neutral in the Galactic Civil War, a
position made possible by the Empire's surpris-
Ingly light touch when dealing with the Bothans.
However, intrigue is the name of the game with
Pbothans; their spynet secretly serves the Reb-
» els, funneling information about virtually every
aspect of the Empire to them. The informa-
» tion ranges from the mundane to ultrasecret
eports on new ships, Imperial activity, and
the highest personnel in Imperial leadership.

Is a near-human strain. So
far, they remain distant

1

Boz Pity: Boz Pity is a graveyard world used by regional
systems to hold their dead. It gained some notoriety dur-
ing the Clone Wars’ Outer Rim Sieges. Otherwise, it has no
living population, though it has drawn attention on occa-
sion from pirates or Rebels seeking to establish bases of
operation. As such, the Empire keeps tabs on the world
to see if more is going on than burial of the dead.

Centares: Centares is an industrial trade world at the
outer edge of Mid Rim space. It is a kickoff point for
those headed into the reaches of the Outer Rim and a
welcome bit of civilization for those returning.

Cerea: Cerea is a relative paradise, peaceful and
remote. Cereans live in harmony with nature, while
visitors reside in technologically enabled compounds.
Though they were pulled into the Clone Wars, the
Cereans remain isolationist and virtually unheard from
under Imperial rule.

Falleen: Falleen is the homeworld of the reclusive spe-
cies of the same name. The Falleen resent the Empire for
using an orbital bombardment to stave off and destroy
a lethal biological agent that escaped from an Imperial
lab. Prince Xizor currently heads the powerful Black Sun
criminal syndicate, and he holds Darth Vader personally
responsible for the bombardment of Falleen.

Iridonia: lridonia is the homeworld of the Zabrak spe-
cies. Due to its extremely harsh environmental condi-
tions, including fiercely powerful storms and _ lethal
seas, many Zabrak have relocated to other worlds.

Ithor: Ithor is a center of botanical and biological com-
merce. Their influence extends throughout the galaxy via
their spacefaring herdships. The Ithorians consider the
surface of their planet sacred, and live in floating herd cit-
les to minimize their impact on it. The Empire intimidated
and coerced the peaceful Ithorians into handing over gen-
erations of scientific and botanical studies.

Kashyyyk: Kashyyyk, the homeworld of the Wook-
lees, is a world of extremely dense forests with trees
thousands of meters tall. The Wookiees live mainly in
the treetops, as Kashyyyk’s ecology tends to become
more lethal the closer one gets to the forest floor. The
Empire officially occupied and enslaved the Wookiees
after the Clone Wars. In the ensuing decades, Wookiee
slave labor has been used in countless Imperial and
corporate projects, including the building of the Death
Star. Kashyyyk is a tightly controlled and highly subju-
gated world.

Malastare: Malastare is an active world on the Hydian
Way. Several sapient species share the world, includ-
ing the Gran and the Dugs. The Gran live in the urban

THE GALAXY
AGE OF REBELLION

areas, while the Dugs live among the trees and wild
lands. The Gran regard and use the Dugs as a labor
force. The Empire placed a human governor in con-
trol of the planet, much to the annoyance of the local
population.

Naboo: Naboo is the homeworld of the Gungans but is
largely ruled by a human government established long
before the creation of the Empire. It is the homeworld
of Emperor Palpatine, who once was its Republic Sena-
tor, as well as Queen Amidala and others who played
significant roles in the Clone Wars and the rise of the
Empire. Naboo, now a subjugated world, houses one
of Palpatine’s private retreats. Despite, or perhaps
because of, its Imperial heritage, Naboo has an active
anti-Imperial resistance movement.

Ord Mantell: Ord Mantell is a haven for scoundrels,
smugglers, and gamblers. It is nearly lawless, and has
been for most of its history. However, it also has a grow-
ing Imperial presence ever on the lookout for covert
Rebel activity and conducting criminal crackdowns.
Due to astrogation complexities, the planet is nearly a
required stopover for those transiting from the Entralla
Route to the Celanon Spur hyperlane.

Roche: Roche is the home system of the insectoid
Verpine species, which lives in a massive asteroid field.
The Empire has virtually enslaved the Verpine for their
expert engineering skills, which fuel the Imperial mili-
tary industrial machine. Despite Imperials stationed
within the asteroid field to keep the Verpine in line, the
Rebels have secretly enlisted Verpine help, most nota-
bly in designing the B-wing starfighter.

Ruusan: Ruusan is an ordinary world in the Mining
Guild with a major role in Jedi history. A millennium
ago, the Sith were essentially destroyed as a major
force in the galaxy for hundreds of years. The Brother-
hood of Darkness was consumed by a thought bomb at
the last battle of Ruusan during the Light and Darkness
War. The location is now known as the Valley of the Jedi.

Ubrikkia: Ubrikkia is a manufacturing center for the
famous Ubrikkian Industries and its subsidiaries.
Repulsorlift vehicles, military craft, droids, and more
come from various Ubrikkian factories.

The Wheel: The Wheel is an aptly named, enormous
wheel-shaped space station located on the Perlemian
Trade Route. It is Known for its casinos and gladiato-
rial arenas. Ships of almost any size can dock with the
station. The Wheel currently enjoys a special kind
of immunity, and Imperial ships are not allowed to
approach. The station pays taxes to the Empire to
maintain this status.


### The Outer Rim Territories

a
i

THE OUTER RIM TERRITORIES

he Outer Rim Territories are the outermost official

regions in Known Space. They are by far the most
expansive and least-explored areas—a wide swath of
space tracing the Rim three-quarters of the way around
the galaxy. The area's extreme distance from Imperial
Center (Coruscant) and the Core Worlds has always
been both a great asset and major hindrance to its
prosperity. As with the Republic before it, the Empire's
control and influence varies greatly from system to
system and from sector to sector. Though the region's
reputation for lawlessness and danger is well-deserved,
this is often simply not the case in the Outer Rim’s
more highly developed systems and sectors.

Despite its remoteness, the Outer Rim has played a
Significant role in galactic politics, economics, and war-
fare since ancient times, well before the origins of the
Republic. The expanding Hutts clashed with the Tion
Hegemony and Xim the Despot before the Perlemian
Trade Route connected Coruscant to the region. Since
that time, exploration and colonization of the Territo-
ries has been irregular and haphazard.

The rate of Outer Rim exploration was closely tied
to the extension of the major hyperlanes into the far-
thest reaches of the galaxy. The earliest develooment
expanded from the Perlemian Trade Route in the north-
eastern quadrant, around the Jedi world of Ossus
and the Tion Hegemony. The presence of Hutt Space
Slowed and deterred colonization of the Slice, driving
colonists to seek worlds away from Hutt influence and
interference. Similarly, difficulties with various Sith and
Mandalorian attacks and invasions struck the develop-

ment of the northern area. Exploration picked up in the
southern reaches with the extension of the Rimma Trade
Route, the Corellian Trade Spine, and the Hydian Way.

Republic control over the Outer Rim has varied
greatly over the ages, with each system often respon-
sible for its own security. Power struggles between
regional factions, isolationist systems, and militant
species all contributed to the difficulties of maintain-
ing order. Beyond that, significant criminal enterprises
found a great haven in the Outer Rim, sometimes offi-
cially sanctioned by corrupt and disreputable govern-
ments and corporations.

OUTER REACHES

Chancellor Palpatine used the Outer Rim as a great
game board in his bid to take control of the Republic.
Using the politics of Outer Rim trade conflicts to propel
himself into the position of Chancellor, Palpatine then
orchestrated the Clone Wars through the creation of the
Separatist movement. This enabled him to oversee the
creation of a powerful central government and military to
counter the growing Separatist threat in the Outer Rim.

The Clone Wars destabilized political control in the
Outer Rim. The Hutts took advantage of the situation
to extend Hutt Space to cover more than three quar-
ters of the Outer Rim portion of the Slice. The Separat-
ists controlled most of the rest, plus about half of the
northern reaches. They also held a large, continuous
region along the Hydian Way and Rimma Trade Route.

THE GALAXY
AGE OF REBELLION

After the Clone Wars, the Empire used its ever-grow-
ing military might to reestablish control over significant
areas in the Outer Rim. The Hutts were pushed back
to their traditional holdings, more or less. Outer Rim
citizens often welcomed the return of civilized trade
and transport. However, some systems and sectors
benefited more than others. Some felt that oppressive
actions were all too common within the Empire. Over
time, Imperial political realities, interference, and mili-
tary interventions started to wear on the independent-
minded Outer Rim residents.

Rebel sympathy is at its highest in the Outer Rim.
However, given its enormously diverse civilizations,
species, and geography, there is no consensus on the
war. Worlds that fare extremely well under the Empire
enthusiastically support the Imperials. For the most

minded Rebel leadership tries to take care regarding
with whom they deal. While dealing with unsavory
characters comes with the job when running a rebel-
lion, agents and recruiters try to steer clear of the
worst offenders and most fearsome crime lords.

NOTABLE LOCATIONS

The following are areas of interest within the Outer
Rim, at or before the rise of the Empire.

Bespin: Bespin is a gas giant and home to the Tibanna
gas mining colony of Cloud City. The city fell under Sep-
aratist control during the Clone Wars, but it now oper-
ates independently. It has popular resorts, casinos, and
major gambling events, making it something of a tour-
ist destination in addition to its mining activities.

remote fringe worlds, little has changed with the rise of
the Empire. They must rely on themselves for their own
security and destiny.

Christophsis: Christophsis is a crystalline planet that
suffered greatly as a major Clone Wars battleground.
Once loyal to the Republic, it is now equally loyal to
the Empire.

Colu (Clak’dor VII): Clak’dor VII is the homeworld of
the Bith species. The planet was ecologically destroyed
by a civil war, forcing the Bith to live within sealed,
domed cities. It was a Separatist world during the time

Rebel recruiting, training, and missions generally
operate with the greatest freedom and effectiveness
against targets in the Outer Rim. The anti-Imperial and
criminal atmosphere make it easier to obtain badly
needed weapons and supplies, though the more noble-

It's a mistake to think that just because the Empire's reach is diminished in much of the Outer Rim, the
people feel free to join the Rebellion. It’s also a mistake to think that just because some of the Rebellion’s
biggest victories and staunchest allies are in the Outer Rim, other systems will follow suit. The Rebellion
uses the Outer Rim for many of the same reasons as everyone else. It’s a long way from the Imperial
strongholds, there are a lot of systems to hide in, and there is no shortage of those looking to avoid or
get back at the Empire. While it's a better recruiting ground than other regions in the galaxy, people do
not automatically sympathize with the Rebellion.

Of course, the Empire also knows all of these reasons and more. They know the Rebellion is out here, they
know we're recruiting, and they're doing everything in their power to prevent that and destroy us at the
same time. You have to be every bit as careful out here as you would in a Coreward region. Maybe more
so, if you don't want to attract too many bounty hunters. Rebels and Rebel sympathizers often mean cred-
its to those people, and the better a Rebel you are, the more you're worth. In fact, while we have to worry
about informants and spies in our operations galaxy-wide, it can be worse out here. A lot of those living in
the Outer Rim are already on shaky moral ground; the opportunity to make a few credits by betraying a few
strangers can be all too tempting. Some are professional criminals, while others are simply selfish scum.

otill, if the danger wasn't worth it, we wouldn't be sending you out. The fact is, we need more people on our
side. If we're going to topple the Emperor, we need more than idealists and the betrayed and the fed-up.
We need entire star systems and sectors, but the only way to get there is to convince one individual at
a time. This is especially true when there’s no major event to influence public opinion in a dramatic way.

The destruction of the Death Star helped. Actually, just the existence of the Death Star helped. Unfortu-
nately, Alderaan did too. Every unjust or just plain evil Imperial act should push more people to our side.
We need to be there to convince them we can win.

of the Clone Wars, but it has subsequently begun to
chafe under Imperial rule.

—

Dantooine: Dantooine, an active world in the ancient
Republic, holds the ruins of an ancient Jedi academy
and training center. Dantooine was home to the Rebel
Alliance's main headquarters until the discovery of an
Imperial tracking device forced a near-instant evacua-
tion and relocation to Yavin 4. Imperials are also active
on Dantooine, primarily as researchers, though they do
watch for any signs of Rebels returning to their aban-
doned base.

Elrood: Elrood is a major Outer Rim commerce and
manufacturing center and the capital of Elrood sec-
tor. It nominally supports the Empire, though many
citizens would prefer to be independent, and it has a
well-deserved reputation for enforcing very strict rules
to maintain order.

Eriadu: Eriadu is the capital world of the Seswenna
sector. It is Grand Moff Tarkin’s homeworld and _ his
influential family’s traditional power base. Eriadu was
loyal to the Republic during the Clone Wars. It has
since become an enormously important Imperial Outer
Rim power base, and the citizenry is extremely pro-
Imperial. Eriadu is more similar to a Core World than
any Outer Rim planet.

Endor: Endor is a virtually unknown, remote system.
The Forest Moon of the gas giant Endor has been com-
pletely isolated by the Empire, though very few in the
galaxy have even noticed.

ys
ae e re

a

Felucia: Felucia is a wild, untamed world with danger-
ous animals and exotic, fungal plants. Felucia was a
major battleground during the Clone Wars. The Empire
maintains small research outposts on the planet.

Gamorr: Gamorr is the homeworld of the Gamorrean
species. The planet is often in a state of war due to the
ever-present squabbling between Gamorrean clans. It
once held a lesser Sith training facility. Imperial efforts
to bring control to the world have largely failed, and
they limit their presence to selected cities and their
own fortified zones.

Gand: Gand is the homeworld of the species of the
same name. It is ruled by a totalitarian monarchy. Gand
settlements are small colonies separated by dense
mists. The environmental conditions led to the creation
of the Gand Findsmen, who hunt fugitives through the
mists. Some individual Gand have joined the Rebel
Alliance.

Geonosis: Geonosis is the ringed homeworld of the
Geonosian species. It is located in close proximity to
the Tatooine system and served as an early base for
the Separatists. It was the site of the first major battle
of the Clone Wars. The Empire has taken control of the
planet's impressive manufacturing facilities.

Hoth: The frozen, inhospitable world of Hoth is largely
ignored by the galaxy, aside from the occasional pirate
Or criminal using it as a base or hideout. See page
478 for more on Hoth.

THE GALAXY

THE EXPANDING
STAR WARS UNIVERSE

his chapter covers the broad basics of the

galaxy as a whole, at the time of the Empire.
By using both Rebel and Imperial viewpoints, it
is meant to show the state of the Galactic Civil
War, as well as introduce new players to the
vast, expansive universe from the movies and
into the stories beyond. It also serves to remind
longtime fans where recognizable worlds and
regions can be found in the galaxy.

At over thirty years old and counting, Star
Wars stories new and old continue to be popu-
lar. With new novels, graphic novels, fictional
reference books, TV shows, and games releas-
ing every year, no single source can keep up.
Future Star Wars roleplaying game products
will, of course, continue to provide more mate-
rial about many areas. Hopefully, this chapter
will inspire Game Masters and players to seek
out more information about locations that stir
their interest. It’s a big universe.

Kessel: Kessel is the location of the legendary Kessel
Run, a treacherous hyperspace route bounded by a
cluster of black holes and used by smugglers to avoid
Imperial patrols. Kessel is an Imperial-controlled prison
planet where glitterstim spice is mined. Illicit trade in
glitterstim is still pervasive, right under the Empire’s
collective nose.

Mon Calamari (Mon Cala, Dac): Mon Calamari is the
homeworld of both the surface-dwelling Mon Calamari
and the subaquatic civilization of the Quarren. This
water-covered world was enslaved by the Empire but
has recently freed itself. It is one of the few openly
rebellious worlds. See page 377 for more on Mon
Calamari (Dac).

Mandalore: Mandalore is the homeworld of the
Mandalorians, an ancient, warrior-based culture. The
Mandalorians once ruled large swaths of the galaxy.
After having suffered their own internal clashes during
the Clone Wars, the Mandalorians struggle to main-
tain their traditional culture and hold little influence
beyond their own system and sector.

Mustafar: Mustafar is an obscure, volcanic world
mined by the Techno Union. Darth Vader killed the last
members of the Separatist Council on Mustafar before
losing to Obi-Wan Kenobi in an epic lightsaber battle.
The planet is currently under Imperial control.

Polis Massa: This system holds the home asteroid
field of the Polis Massans and is mined via attached
stations. It was the secret birthplace of Luke Skywalker
and Leia Organa.

THE GALAXY
AGE OF REBELLION

=

Rodia: Rodia, in the Tyrius system, is the homeworld
of the Rodian species. It is a jungle-covered planet,
with huge environmental shields protecting major cit-
les from their surroundings. Clan politics dominate
Rodian life, which is typically violent. The Rodians are
far too interested in their own struggles to be overly
concerned about Imperial control.

Ryloth: Ryloth is the homeworld of the Twi'lek species.
The planet is subject to radical weather and rotational
patterns, which produce heat storms that warm the
world. Many citizens live underground. The Empire
controls Ryloth.

Socorro: Socorro is desert world featuring a popular
shadowport, millions of inhabitants, and no organized
government.

Sluis Van: Sluis Van is the homeworld of the Sluissi, a
species famous for their technical and starship skills.
The world is a major Imperial shipyard, heavily guarded
by a series of battle stations.

Sullust: Sullust is the homeworld of the Sullustans. It is
dominated by the SoroSuub corporation, which is in league
with the Empire. See page 480 for more on Sullust.

Taris: Taris is a city-planet (ecumenopolis) that held a
central role in the ancient Jedi, Sith, and Mandalorian
wars. The planet’s influence and stature has diminished
over time, and it is now under Imperial control.

Tatooine: Tatooine is the homeworld of the Jawa and
Sand People (Tusken Raider) species. It is a desert
world, and is either the origin or homeworld of many
influential figures in the galaxy’s recent history. It is
under Imperial control after having being wrested from
the Hutts following the Clone Wars. Jabba the Hutt and
his illegal operations remain a dominant force across
Tatooine,

Teth: The Empire took over Teth and turned it into the
capital of Baxel sector and, nominally, Hutt Space. The
Hutts reestablished their influence through their own
underhanded methods, and as such, Teth’s Imperial
Moff is assumed to be corrupt.

Telos: Destroyed thousands of years ago by a Sith fleet
and rebuilt by the Republic, Telos’s civilian population
has suffered atrocities under Imperial rule.

Utapau: Utapau is the homeworld of two intelligent spe-
cies, the Pau’ans and the Utai. The world is under Impe-
rial control, after once having served as a secret location
for the Separatist Council during the Clone Wars.

Yavin: Yavin is the gas giant that the moon Yavin 4 orbits
and is named for. It was the location of the Rebellion’s
most important secret Rebel base. See page 381 for
more information on Yavin.

Ziost: A central world for the ancient Sith, Ziost was
the site of numerous battles and power struggles. After
the Clone Wars, the system was purged from Imperial
navigation charts and other information stores.


### Wild Space

WILD SPACE

W ld Space has long been the term for minimally
explored areas at the fringes of Known Space. Most
of Wild Space appears at or near the outer sections of
the Outer Rim, but pockets of nominally explored space
may be found almost anywhere in the galaxy. Due to
continuous efforts to explore and colonize system after
system, Wild Space is the ever-changing edge of the
map. Wild Space represents great opportunities to inde-
pendent, corporate, and government scouts. Discover-
ing a new civilization with which to trade, or a new world
rich in precious metals and ore, can make the discoverer
famous. While the scouts might get a substantial bonus
for a great find, the real credits are to be made by the
traders and developers to follow.

Wild Space is dangerous. Just getting into the area can
be tricky due to nonexistent or outdated astrogation data.
Initial contact with a new species rarely goes smoothly,
and most scouts arent fully equipped diplomatically or

WILDLY UNKNOWN

We appreciate your desire to help the Rebel Al-
liance. While your scout ship isn't well-suited to
combat or clandestine work, we do have a mission
for which you—and your ship—are uniquely quali-
fied. We don't get to do this very often, so even
though you won't be taking on the Empire directly,
you will be helping the Alliance tremendously.

We want to send you on an exploration mission.
We have a collection of unexplored or underex-
plored areas we hope are hiding worlds suit-
able for a new base, or a new Safe world. Basi-
cally, somewhere the Empire doesn’t know about.
Somewhere nice is preferred. Somewhere livable
is acceptable. Anything too entertaining, well,
wed love to see some holos of bizarre planets,
but let's not get carried away,

Now, some of these areas are in Wild Space,
some are in the Unknown Regions. Hit Wild Space
first. It’s simply unexplored, and relatively easy to
get into and out of—aside from the sketchy navi-
gational data. For the Unknown Regions—well, |
hope youre a great navigator. These areas are
right along the border, and they'll still make your
navicomputer smoke and whimper.

That reminds me. Report in before you enter the
Unknown Regions. We'll want some clue where you
went if we need to send someone after you...as-
suming we can find someone crazy enough to follow.

—Rebel! Briefing Officer

Officially to deal with the situation. Plenty of those worlds
may be registered as uninhabited or unknown, but scouts
never know when a lost colony, smuggler’s den, or under-
world base might suddenly appear. Such settlements are
rarely happy to be discovered.

During the Clone Wars, Wild Space made for con-
venient hiding places and staging areas for the Sepa-
ratists. Whether through ties to mega-corporations or
to the various industrial and mining guilds, they had
a wealth of proprietary scouting information unavail-
able to the Republic. Both during the war and in the
dark times afterward, desperate refugees sometimes
fled into Wild Space. Now Imperial scouts search the
regions for new territory and any sign of Rebel activity.
Rebels too search Wild Space for suitable planets for
bases or safe worlds.

NOTABLE LOCATIONS

The following are areas of interest within Wild Space, at
or before the rise of the Empire.

Bakura: Though colonized a century and a half ago,
remote Bakura remained largely isolated from the gal-
axy. The original colonists believed in the Cosmic Bal-
ance, and this belief continues to the modern day. Not
long after the Battle of Yavin, an Imperial fleet forced
Bakura to join the Empire. Bakura manufactures repul-
sorlift coils, which interests the Imperials.

Indoumodo: This lush world is mainly known as the
homeworld of the poisonous kouhun creatures. The
world is located near the end of the Corellian Run.
Assassins and other poisoners travel to the world to
gather the creatures for use as untraceable killing
machines.

Kamino: The homeworld of the Kaminoans, this was the
source of the Republic's clone armies during the Clone
Wars. The water-covered world is still under strict Impe-
rial control. Kamino is an extragalactic system located in
an area of Wild Space beyond the Outer Rim, in a dwarf
satellite galaxy near the Rishi Maze.

Kesh: This world holds an ancient, long-lost colony of
sith. After accidentally crashing on the planet in the
time of the Old Republic, the survivors managed to set
up their own civilization, which evolved and expanded
over the centuries. No government, corporation, or
other official entity is aware of Kesh’s existence.

Mytus: Mytus is in a section of Wild Space beyond
the Corporate Sector and Trianii Space, on a secret
hyperspace route from Farana. Mytus holds Star’s End
prison, which houses prisoners in stasis. The prison was
heavily damaged several years ago during a “visit” by
Han Solo and Chewbacca.


### The Unknown Regions

THE UNKNOWN REGIONS

he “Unknown Regions’ is the official term for unex-

plored space beyond the Known Worlds. As such,
this area continually shrinks in size as civilization—origi-
nally the Republic and now the Empire—has expanded
through the ages. In modern times, the term most often
applies to the unexplored western side of the galaxy,
naturally barred from normal hyperspace travel. The
area iS SO massive that it could hide entire interstellar
civilizations that have never contacted each other, much
less the rest of the galaxy.

The Unknown Regions remain a source of outrageous
tales and sinister threats. Galactic civilization views the
area aS a combination of romantic frontier and a source
of unexpected horror. It is a place that has produced
new threats and reinvigorated old enemies for millennia.
Before the establishment of the Republic, in the earli-
est days of Coruscant, the Rakata Infinite Empire ruled
worlds across the galaxy from their homeworld deep
In the modern-day Unknown Regions. During the time
of the Old Republic, Sith and Mandalorian foes long
thought destroyed would periodically reemerge from
the Unknown Regions and wreak havoc on the galaxy.

The modern-day boundaries of the Unknown Regions
were established with the discovery of a great tangle
of hyperspace anomalies forming a barrier that splits
the galaxy virtually in two. The tangle makes hyper-
space travel extremely difficult or impossible. Though
its origins are debatable, it has been a natural obstacle
to galactic exploration and development for millen-
nia. Survey missions and other efforts to find reliable
routes are at best slow and dangerous.

However, passage is not completely impossible. The
Republic sent a well-supported, long-term expedition
In the form of the Outbound Flight project. Like the
majority of such efforts, it ended in disappearance
and disaster. Travelers also occasionally emerge from
the other side, though their arrival is often violent and
ageressive.

During the Clone Wars, official exploration efforts
largely ceased, with Republic scouts distracted by the
war. Government-sponsored exploration began anew
with the Empire, in an effort to expand Imperial space,
exploit new resources or worlds, and search for any
sign of Rebel activity.

oF

NOTABLE LOCATIONS

The following are areas of interest within the Unknown
Regions, at or before the rise of the Empire.

Csilla: Homeworld of the Chiss and capital of the Chiss
Ascendancy, an enormous empire concealed within
the Unknown Regions. Grand Admiral Thrawn, formerly
of the Chiss Ascendancy and now Serving the Galactic
Empire, is currently leading a secret mission to map
the Unknown Regions and protect the Empire from any
unexpected threats.

llum: Located in the Unknown Regions in the north-
west quadrant of the galaxy, Ilum is a world originally
held secret by the Jedi. Under the mountains of this
icy world are llum crystals. Jedi use these crystals in
lightsabers, which are responsible for the blue or green
glow of the blade. The world is now monitored by Impe-
rial droids, who keep a watchful eye for any surviving
Jedi or other Force users returning for the crystals.

Killik Colony Worlds: Originally from prehistoric
Alderaan, the insectoid, hive-mind Killik species is
among the most ancient in the galaxy. The Killiks relo-
cated to the Unknown Regions tens of thousands of
years ago, and reside on several colony worlds border-
ing Chiss space, although their old mounds can been
seen on worlds throughout the galaxy such as Lothal
and Utapau.

The Redoubt: For centuries, the Chiss have studied
the globular cluster they call the Redoubt. As the name
suggests, they consider it useful as a well-defended
retreat, should they ever flee their own worlds. It is also
the as-yet-unknown final resting spot for the long-lost
Outbound Flight, which disappeared during the Clone
Wars while on a mission to attempt extragalactic travel
and study.

Unknown World (Rakata Prime, Lehon): This home-
world of the Rakata species was the center of their
ancient Infinite Empire, which once spanned the gal-
axy. Now, however, the planet is long abandoned,
save for the relics and ruins that dot the beaches and
oceans of its surface.


### Rebel Territory

REBEL TERRITORY

here is very little actual Rebel territory in the galaxy.

There are a few star systems, such as Mon Calamari,
that openly support the Rebellion, but these are very few
and far between. Typically, a system that openly supports
the Alliance must be remote, well-defended, and yet
unimportant enough on the galactic stage that the effort
of retaking the system is not worth the cost. Systems that
turn from insignificant problems to political or military
threats or embarrassments suddenly find they are worth
the effort to be mercilessly crushed. The early Seccession
Worlds learned this all too well (see the History of the
Rebellion, page 484). To avoid this fate, sympathetic
systems prefer to help the Rebellion behind the scenes.
They donate labor, starships, materials, equipment, and
whatever else they can contribute.

Conquering and holding territory is not a strategy
the Rebellion wants, or can afford, to utilize. The Rebel
Alliance is simply too small, and its military is only a
fraction of the size needed to succeed in a brute force
attack. The Rebels prefer to convince dissident star
systems to join them in other ways.

However, the Rebels do hold small amounts of terri-
tory, whether these are secret bases, protected lands
on sympathetic worlds, space stations, or secret safe
worlds. These areas give the Rebels sanctuaries in which
to recover, plan, and train without Imperial interference.
Some of these are heavily guarded and able to defend
themselves in the face of Imperial attack.

Life in Rebel territory is one of at least temporary
Safety and reprieve from Imperial forces and laws,
Every Rebel knows they are just one mistake or Impe-
rial informant away from a Star Destroyer arriving to
ruin the day, but they have become used to the every-
day strain such worry causes.

FLASHPOINTS

As the Rebellion expands into new territories and sys-
tems, some worlds naturally attract more Rebel atten-
tion than others. Areas with a lot of activity tend to
have both a population open to Rebel support and
important Imperial facilities and worthwhile targets.
Areas with sudden increases in Rebel attacks and
interference are considered flashpoints. They are often
the front lines of military clashes between the Rebels
and the Empire. The Battle of Yavin and subsequent
blockade made its area a flashpoint. The areas in the
immediate vicinity of Bestine and Mon Calamari are
flashpoints. Repeated targets of starfighter strikes are
most often flashpoints.

SAFE WORLDS

The existence and location of Rebel safe worlds are
among the best kept secrets in the Alliance. Safe
worlds offer sanctuary to those who cannot remain
in Imperial space, or anywhere Imperial agents might
locate them. Residents of safe worlds are families and
friends of active members of the Rebel Alliance who
might face arrest, imprisonment, or worse from the
Imperials in retaliation for their association with the
member in question. Safe worlds not only protect, but
